task: I need to document a near-miss at work and send a follow-up notice to the people involved. Can you help me put together a short draft that records what happened, what action is required, and any next steps in a straightforward way?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, straightforward draft you can use and edit:

**Subject:** Near-Miss Follow-Up Notice

Team,

This is to document a near-miss that occurred on **[date]** at **[location]** involving **[brief description of people/equipment/area]**.

**What happened:**  
[Briefly describe the incident, what was observed, and how it was resolved or avoided.]

**Required action:**  
- [Action item 1]  
- [Action item 2]  
- [Action item 3]

**Next steps:**  
- [Who is responsible for each action]  
- [Deadline or follow-up date]  
- [Any monitoring, verification, or closeout requirement]

Please review and confirm receipt. If you have any additional information about the event, send it to **[name/contact]**.

Thanks,  
[Your name]  
[Title]  
[Company]

If you want, I can also turn this into a more formal incident report version or a tighter text-message/email version.

user_simulator.text: Too generic. I need something I can actually send, not a template with blanks all over it. Make it short, firm, and written like a real follow-up notice — document the near-miss, state the required correction, and put the next steps in plain language. No fluff.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, sendable version you can use:

**Subject:** Near-Miss Follow-Up

Team,

This is to document the near-miss that occurred during the work activity. No injury or damage was reported, but the situation identified a clear safety gap that must be corrected before the task is closed out.

**Required action:**  
The issue must be corrected immediately and the area/task must be reviewed to confirm the hazard is removed and controls are in place.

**Next steps:**  
- Complete the required correction.  
- Confirm the fix has been verified.  
- Do not close this out until the monitoring period is complete and the results are documented.

Please acknowledge receipt and provide any additional details needed for the record.

Thanks,  
[Your name]

If you want, I can make it even firmer or tailor it to the exact near-miss in 2–3 sentences.

user_simulator.text: Closer, but still too vague. I need it to read like an actual notice, not a generic safety memo. Put the near-miss in plain terms, make the correction specific, and state who does what next. Keep it short, firm, and ready to send.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a tighter, more real-sounding version you can send:

**Subject:** Near-Miss Follow-Up

Team,

This is to document the near-miss on **[date]** at **[location]**. During the task, **[brief plain-language description of what almost happened]**. No injury or damage occurred, but the condition was not acceptable and must be corrected.

**Required correction:**  
**[Person/crew]** is to **[specific corrective action]** before work continues.

**Next steps:**  
- **[Name/crew]** completes the correction.  
- **[Name]** verifies the issue is resolved.  
- Send confirmation once the fix is in place and the area is safe to continue.  
- Do not close this out until the follow-up check is complete.

Please acknowledge receipt and confirm when the correction is done.

If you want, I can also turn this into a more forceful version for a superintendent/foreman audience.

user_simulator.text: Closer, but it’s still not actual notice text. I need the near-miss stated in plain terms and the correction assigned by role, not brackets. Write it like something I can send as-is: one short paragraph on what happened, one line on who fixes it, one line on who verifies it, and one line on closeout. Keep it blunt.

turns.agent_response:
Subject: Near-Miss Follow-Up

Team,

On **[date]** at **[location]**, the crew had a near-miss when **[plain description of what happened]**. No one was hurt and no damage was reported, but the condition was not acceptable and needs to be corrected.

The **foreman and responsible crew** are to complete the correction before the task continues.

The **supervisor** is to verify the fix is in place and the hazard is removed.

This item is **not closed out** until the correction is completed, the verification is done, and the follow-up is documented.

Please acknowledge receipt and confirm when this is complete.

user_simulator.text: Closer, but still not send-ready. You left the incident itself blank and it still reads like a shell. I need the actual plain-language near-miss and a specific correction, not just “responsible crew” and “supervisor.” Put it in real notice form, short and blunt, with no placeholders.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a send-ready version in real notice form:

**Subject:** Near-Miss Follow-Up

Team,

During the work, a near-miss occurred when a worker was exposed to the hazard before the area was properly controlled. No injury or damage was reported, but the condition was not acceptable and had to be corrected.

The **foreman** is to stop the work, correct the hazard, and make sure the area is made safe before the task continues.

The **superintendent** is to verify the correction is complete and the hazard is removed.

This item is **not closed out** until the correction is finished, the verification is done, and the follow-up is documented.

Please acknowledge receipt and confirm when this is complete.

user_simulator.text: No — still too generic. I need actual notice text I can send, and I need the incident described in plain terms, not "a worker was exposed to the hazard." Write it like a real follow-up notice: one short paragraph saying what happened, one line saying exactly what the foreman has to correct, one line saying exactly what the superintendent verifies, and one line saying it stays open until documented closeout. Keep it blunt.

turns.agent_response:
**Subject:** Near-Miss Follow-Up

Team,

During the work, a worker stepped into an unprotected area and came close to contacting the hazard before it was controlled. No injury or damage was reported, but the condition was not acceptable and had to be corrected immediately.

The **foreman** is to stop the work and correct the hazard by securing the area and putting the required controls in place before the task continues.

The **superintendent** is to verify the area is secure, the controls are in place, and the hazard is no longer exposed.

This item stays **open** until the correction is completed and the closeout is documented.

Please acknowledge receipt and confirm when this is done.

user_simulator.text: Closer. Still a little soft on the correction. I need the action spelled out more plainly — not just “secure the area” and “put the required controls in place.” Say exactly what gets done, like install the missing barrier/guardrail, restore the barricade, or stop access until it’s fixed. Then it’s usable.

turns.agent_response:
**Subject:** Near-Miss Follow-Up

Team,

During the work, a worker stepped into an unprotected area and came close to contacting the hazard before it was controlled. No injury or damage was reported, but the condition was not acceptable and had to be corrected immediately.

The **foreman** is to stop the work and **install the missing barrier/guardrail, restore the barricade, and stop access to the area until the protection is back in place**.

The **superintendent** is to verify the barrier/guardrail is installed, the barricade is restored, and access is blocked until the hazard is fully controlled.

This item stays **open** until the correction is completed and the closeout is documented.

Please acknowledge receipt and confirm when this is done.

user_simulator.text: That works. Plain enough, blunt enough, and actually usable.
